task_completion.visual_evidence: A dive logbook application (e.g., Subsurface) is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Identify and select a dive site that is missing GPS coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent has selected a dive site (such as Sund Rock, Zenobia, or Green Bay) in the application, and its GPS/coordinates field is shown to be initially empty.

task_completion.description: Update the dive site with the target GPS coordinates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The coordinates '47.39170°N, 122.57694°W' (or the decimal equivalent '47.3917, -122.5769') are typed or pasted into the coordinates/location input field for the selected dive site.

task_completion.description: Apply the coordinate updates to the dive site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks an 'Apply', 'Save', or checkmark button within the dive site edit panel to confirm the new coordinates.
The agent clicks the main 'Save' icon, navigates to 'File -> Save', or the application window title updates to show there are no unsaved changes (e.g., an asterisk disappears).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the graphical dive log application rather than editing the raw file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Subsurface (or similar) GUI being used. The agent must not be using a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to manually edit the XML/SSRF file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'sed', 'awk', or python scripts being executed to modify the dive log file directly.

integrity.description: The agent navigated the application menus and fields naturally.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical progression of opening the app, finding the site, editing the field, and saving, rather than the file magically updating in the background.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target latitude is 47.3917. Target longitude is -122.5769.
